sequences.label: Sequences
sequences.deep_explanation: Sequences is a foundational topic in calculus. Convergence, divergence, bounded sequences
sequences.exam_tip: Practice Sequences problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

series.label: Infinite Series
series.deep_explanation: Infinite Series is a foundational topic in calculus. Convergence tests, power series, Taylor/Maclaurin
series.exam_tip: Practice Infinite Series problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

limits.label: Limits
limits.canonical_definition: Limit evaluation, L'Hôpital's rule, indeterminate forms
limits.deep_explanation: Limits is a foundational topic in calculus. Limit evaluation, L'Hôpital's rule, indeterminate forms
common_misconceptions: Students substitute x = a directly when the result is 0/0 because they don't recognize indeterminate forms
Students apply L'Hôpital's rule to limits that aren't 0/0 or ∞/∞ because they overuse the tool
Students conflate limit at infinity with limit at a point because both use the same notation lim
Students take one-sided limits as equal without checking because two-sided limits are the usual case
Students forget that limits can exist where the function value doesn't because they expect f(a) and lim(x→a) f(x) to always agree
limits.exam_tip: Practice Limits problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

continuity.label: Continuity
continuity.canonical_definition: Types of discontinuity, intermediate value theorem
continuity.deep_explanation: Continuity is a foundational topic in calculus. Types of discontinuity, intermediate value theorem
common_misconceptions: Students check only lim(x→a) f(x) = f(a) but forget f(a) must exist because they focus on the limit side
Students confuse removable discontinuities with jump discontinuities because both break 'smooth' graphs
Students think piecewise functions are automatically discontinuous at the join because they don't check the match
Students assert continuity from a graph that looks smooth because visual estimation isn't rigorous
Students apply the IVT to discontinuous functions because they don't check the hypothesis
continuity.exam_tip: Practice Continuity problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

differentiability.label: Differentiability
differentiability.deep_explanation: Differentiability is a foundational topic in calculus. Differentiability vs continuity, piecewise functions
common_misconceptions: Students assume continuity implies differentiability because the converse (differentiable ⇒ continuous) is easy to remember backward
Students differentiate across corner points like |x| at x=0 because the function is continuous there
Students use the derivative formula inside an absolute value without checking sign because they don't split cases
Students forget that vertical tangents are a form of non-differentiability because the function is still 'smooth'
Students conflate f'(a) existing with f being differentiable on an interval because they mix pointwise with global
differentiability.exam_tip: Practice Differentiability problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

derivatives-basic.label: Basic Derivatives
derivatives-basic.deep_explanation: Basic Derivatives is a foundational topic in calculus. Power rule, sum/difference, basic functions
common_misconceptions: Students forget the '+C' when writing antiderivatives because the constant feels optional
Students apply the power rule to constants as '0·x⁻¹' instead of 0 because they don't track the special case
Students differentiate constants using the power rule and get 0·x⁻¹ (undefined) because they don't apply d/dx(c) = 0 first
Students mix up d/dx(eˣ) = eˣ with d/dx(xᵉ) = e·xᵉ⁻¹ because the forms look similar
Students forget that d/dx(sin(x)) is cos(x) not -cos(x) because both sine and cosine have alternating-sign derivatives
derivatives-basic.exam_tip: Practice Basic Derivatives problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

chain-rule.label: Chain Rule
chain-rule.deep_explanation: Chain Rule is a foundational topic in calculus. Composite function differentiation
common_misconceptions: Students differentiate f(g(x)) as f'(x)·g'(x) because they forget the inner function's argument
Students apply the chain rule to non-composite functions because they over-apply the pattern
Students stop after one level of chain in f(g(h(x))) because they only see two functions
Students forget to differentiate the inner function because visually it looks 'done' after step one
Students apply d/dx to both sides of an equation without the chain rule (implicit differentiation) because they treat y as a constant
chain-rule.exam_tip: Practice Chain Rule problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

product-quotient-rule.label: Product & Quotient Rule
product-quotient-rule.canonical_definition: Product rule, quotient rule, combined applications
product-quotient-rule.deep_explanation: Product & Quotient Rule is a foundational topic in calculus. Product rule, quotient rule, combined applications
product-quotient-rule.exam_tip: Practice Product & Quotient Rule problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

implicit-differentiation.label: Implicit Differentiation
implicit-differentiation.deep_explanation: Implicit Differentiation is a foundational topic in calculus. Implicit functions, related rates
implicit-differentiation.exam_tip: Practice Implicit Differentiation problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

maxima-minima.label: Maxima & Minima
maxima-minima.canonical_definition: Critical points, second derivative test, optimization
maxima-minima.deep_explanation: Maxima & Minima is a foundational topic in calculus. Critical points, second derivative test, optimization
maxima-minima.exam_tip: Practice Maxima & Minima problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

mean-value-theorems.label: Mean Value Theorems
mean-value-theorems.canonical_definition: Rolle's theorem, Lagrange MVT, Cauchy MVT
mean-value-theorems.deep_explanation: Mean Value Theorems is a foundational topic in calculus. Rolle's theorem, Lagrange MVT, Cauchy MVT
mean-value-theorems.exam_tip: Practice Mean Value Theorems problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

integration-basics.label: Integration Basics
integration-basics.deep_explanation: Integration Basics is a foundational topic in calculus. Antiderivatives, basic integration formulas
common_misconceptions: Students integrate ∫(f·g) dx as (∫f)·(∫g) because they pattern-match on addition-distributes rules
Students forget to add the constant of integration because definite integrals don't need it
Students split ∫dx/(x² + 1) as tan⁻¹(x²+1) because they mis-identify the antiderivative pattern
Students apply u-substitution but forget to change the limits of integration because they keep original bounds
Students integrate cos²(x) by writing (cos(x))²/2 because they treat it like the power rule
integration-basics.exam_tip: Practice Integration Basics problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

integration-substitution.label: Integration by Substitution
integration-substitution.deep_explanation: Integration by Substitution is a foundational topic in calculus. u-substitution, trigonometric substitution
integration-substitution.exam_tip: Practice Integration by Substitution problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

integration-by-parts.label: Integration by Parts
integration-by-parts.canonical_definition: LIATE rule, repeated integration by parts
integration-by-parts.deep_explanation: Integration by Parts is a foundational topic in calculus. LIATE rule, repeated integration by parts
common_misconceptions: Students pick u and dv at random because they don't follow the LIATE heuristic
Students forget to apply integration by parts recursively when the new integral is still complex because one pass feels sufficient
Students drop the boundary term uv evaluated at limits because they focus on the integral piece
Students swap the sign and write ∫u dv = uv + ∫v du because they miscopy the formula
Students use integration by parts on integrals that need substitution because they over-apply the technique
integration-by-parts.exam_tip: Practice Integration by Parts problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

partial-fractions.label: Partial Fractions
partial-fractions.deep_explanation: Partial Fractions is a foundational topic in calculus. Decomposition for rational functions
partial-fractions.exam_tip: Practice Partial Fractions problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

definite-integrals.label: Definite Integrals
definite-integrals.deep_explanation: Definite Integrals is a foundational topic in calculus. FTC, properties, area computation
common_misconceptions: Students compute F(a) - F(b) instead of F(b) - F(a) because they reverse the bounds
Students ignore the sign of the integrand and report area as always positive because they expect area to be non-negative
Students change variable but keep the original limits because they forget to transform the bounds
Students split an integral at a point of discontinuity but compute it as if continuous because they don't notice the discontinuity
Students apply the fundamental theorem of calculus when F is not continuous on [a,b] because they don't verify the hypothesis
definite-integrals.exam_tip: Practice Definite Integrals problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

improper-integrals.label: Improper Integrals
improper-integrals.deep_explanation: Improper Integrals is a foundational topic in calculus. Infinite limits, convergence of integrals
improper-integrals.exam_tip: Practice Improper Integrals problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

multivariable-calculus.label: Multivariable Calculus
multivariable-calculus.deep_explanation: Multivariable Calculus is a foundational topic in calculus. Partial derivatives, total derivative, Jacobian
common_misconceptions: Students treat all variables as functions of x when computing ∂/∂x because they confuse total with partial derivatives
Students forget to hold other variables constant because they're used to single-variable calculus
Students compute ∂²f/∂x∂y and ∂²f/∂y∂x and get different answers because they make algebra errors, not because Clairaut fails
Students apply the chain rule in partials without accounting for all paths because they miss intermediate variables
Students confuse ∂z/∂x with dz/dx because the notation looks similar
multivariable-calculus.exam_tip: Practice Multivariable Calculus problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

multiple-integrals.label: Multiple Integrals
multiple-integrals.deep_explanation: Multiple Integrals is a foundational topic in calculus. Double/triple integrals, change of variables
multiple-integrals.exam_tip: Practice Multiple Integrals problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

matrix-operations.label: Matrix Operations
matrix-operations.deep_explanation: Matrix Operations is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Addition, multiplication, transpose, properties
common_misconceptions: Students multiply matrices element-wise because they confuse matrix multiplication with Hadamard product
Students assume AB = BA because real-number multiplication commutes
Students compute A⁻¹ by inverting each entry because inversion feels like 'the reverse of' multiplication
Students check if a product is defined by counting entries because they don't track rows × columns dimensions
Students apply transpose distribution as (AB)ᵀ = AᵀBᵀ because they expect the order to stay the same
matrix-operations.exam_tip: Practice Matrix Operations problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

determinants.label: Determinants
determinants.deep_explanation: Determinants is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Computation, properties, cofactor expansion
common_misconceptions: Students compute determinants by expanding along the wrong row/column because they don't pick the row or column with the most zeros
Students apply the det(AB) = det(A)·det(B) rule to non-square matrices because they memorize without checking preconditions
Students think det(A + B) = det(A) + det(B) because sums usually distribute over operations
Students compute det(kA) as k·det(A) because scalar multiplication usually commutes with linear operations
Students forget that swapping two rows negates the determinant because they only remember the magnitude
determinants.exam_tip: Practice Determinants problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

matrix-inverse.label: Matrix Inverse
matrix-inverse.deep_explanation: Matrix Inverse is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Inverse computation, adjugate method, properties
matrix-inverse.exam_tip: Practice Matrix Inverse problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

systems-of-equations.label: Systems of Linear Equations
systems-of-equations.deep_explanation: Systems of Linear Equations is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Gaussian elimination, consistency, solution types
common_misconceptions: Students use Cramer's rule when det = 0 because they don't check the coefficient matrix first
Students treat any system with more equations than unknowns as inconsistent because they expect 'too many' equations to conflict
Students back-substitute before reaching row-echelon form because they try to solve partway through elimination
Students confuse 'no solution' with 'infinite solutions' because both produce rows of zeros in reduction
Students forget to multiply through by the pivot before adding rows because they memorize the procedure by rote
systems-of-equations.exam_tip: Practice Systems of Linear Equations problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

rank-nullity.label: Rank & Nullity
rank-nullity.canonical_definition: Row echelon, rank, nullity, rank-nullity theorem
rank-nullity.deep_explanation: Rank & Nullity is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Row echelon, rank, nullity, rank-nullity theorem
common_misconceptions: Students count nonzero rows in the original matrix instead of the row-echelon form because they skip the reduction step
Students confuse rank with determinant because both 'describe' the matrix as a whole
Students think rank + nullity equals the number of rows because they misremember which dimension is being summed
Students apply row reduction inconsistently and miscount pivots because they treat each row independently
Students forget that rank(AB) ≤ min(rank(A), rank(B)) because they expect ranks to add or multiply
rank-nullity.exam_tip: Practice Rank & Nullity problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

vector-spaces.label: Vector Spaces
vector-spaces.deep_explanation: Vector Spaces is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Subspaces, span, basis, dimension
vector-spaces.exam_tip: Practice Vector Spaces problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

linear-transformations.label: Linear Transformations
linear-transformations.deep_explanation: Linear Transformations is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Kernel, image, matrix representation
linear-transformations.exam_tip: Practice Linear Transformations problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

eigenvalues.label: Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors
eigenvalues.deep_explanation: Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Characteristic polynomial, computation, properties
common_misconceptions: Students compute det(A - λI) but forget to solve for λ because they stop once they see a polynomial
Students conflate algebraic multiplicity with geometric multiplicity because both are called 'multiplicity'
Students assume every matrix has distinct eigenvalues because their early examples always did
Students treat eigenvectors as unique vectors because they don't recognize scalar multiples of an eigenvector are also eigenvectors
Students forget that complex eigenvalues come in conjugate pairs for real matrices because they only practiced with symmetric matrices
eigenvalues.exam_tip: Practice Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

diagonalization.label: Diagonalization
diagonalization.deep_explanation: Diagonalization is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Diagonalizability, similar matrices, powers
common_misconceptions: Students try to diagonalize a non-diagonalizable matrix because they don't check algebraic vs geometric multiplicity
Students compute PDP⁻¹ but forget to order eigenvalues and eigenvectors consistently because the pairing is easy to mix up
Students think all symmetric matrices need Gram-Schmidt because they conflate symmetric with orthogonal diagonalization
Students write P as rows of eigenvectors instead of columns because they confuse the convention
Students compute A^n by raising entries to the n-th power because they forget diagonalization transforms the problem
diagonalization.exam_tip: Practice Diagonalization problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

cayley-hamilton.label: Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
cayley-hamilton.deep_explanation: Cayley-Hamilton Theorem is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Statement, applications, matrix powers/inverse
cayley-hamilton.exam_tip: Practice Cayley-Hamilton Theorem problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

orthogonality.label: Orthogonality
orthogonality.deep_explanation: Orthogonality is a foundational topic in linear algebra. Gram-Schmidt, orthogonal matrices, projections
orthogonality.exam_tip: Practice Orthogonality problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-first-order.label: First Order ODEs
ode-first-order.deep_explanation: First Order ODEs is a foundational topic in differential equations. Separable, exact, linear first-order
common_misconceptions: Students forget to multiply BOTH sides by the integrating factor because they treat the equation as an identity
Students compute the integrating factor as e^P(x) instead of e^∫P(x)dx because they skip the integral
Students drop the constant of integration on the integrating factor because it simplifies to 1 anyway
Students apply the first-order-linear method to nonlinear equations because they pattern-match on the form y' + P(x)y = Q(x)
Students forget that the solution must be continuous on the interval of interest because they solve piece-by-piece
ode-first-order.exam_tip: Practice First Order ODEs problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-bernoulli.label: Bernoulli Equations
ode-bernoulli.deep_explanation: Bernoulli Equations is a foundational topic in differential equations. Reduction to linear via substitution
ode-bernoulli.exam_tip: Practice Bernoulli Equations problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-exact.label: Exact Equations
ode-exact.deep_explanation: Exact Equations is a foundational topic in differential equations. Exactness condition, integrating factors
ode-exact.exam_tip: Practice Exact Equations problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-second-order-homo.label: Second Order Homogeneous
ode-second-order-homo.deep_explanation: Second Order Homogeneous is a foundational topic in differential equations. Characteristic equation, complementary solution
common_misconceptions: Students write only one solution for repeated roots because they forget the te^(rt) term
Students use the wrong form for complex roots because they don't convert to e^(αx)(cos(βx) + sin(βx))
Students mix up the characteristic equation form because they confuse ay'' + by' + cy = 0 with y'' + by' + cy = 0
Students assert that the general solution has only two constants because they forget higher-order equations need more
Students apply constant-coefficient methods to variable-coefficient equations because the surface structure looks the same
ode-second-order-homo.exam_tip: Practice Second Order Homogeneous problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-second-order-nonhomo.label: Second Order Non-Homogeneous
ode-second-order-nonhomo.canonical_definition: Particular solution, undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters
ode-second-order-nonhomo.deep_explanation: Second Order Non-Homogeneous is a foundational topic in differential equations. Particular solution, undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters
common_misconceptions: Students try undetermined coefficients with a forcing term that duplicates a homogeneous solution because they don't multiply by x
Students forget that the general solution is the sum of complementary and particular solutions because they produce only one
Students assume variation of parameters always works better than undetermined coefficients because more general sounds better
Students pick the wrong form for the particular solution because they don't match the forcing function structure
Students apply superposition to nonlinear equations because linear-combination habit is strong
prerequisite_reminders: Review ode second order homo first.
ode-second-order-nonhomo.exam_tip: Practice Second Order Non-Homogeneous problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

ode-higher-order.label: Higher Order ODEs
ode-higher-order.deep_explanation: Higher Order ODEs is a foundational topic in differential equations. nth order linear, operator methods
prerequisite_reminders: Review ode second order nonhomo first.
ode-higher-order.exam_tip: Practice Higher Order ODEs problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

pde-basics.label: PDE Basics
pde-basics.deep_explanation: PDE Basics is a foundational topic in differential equations. Classification, separation of variables, wave/heat/Laplace
prerequisite_reminders: Review ode second order homo first.
pde-basics.exam_tip: Practice PDE Basics problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

counting-principles.label: Counting Principles
counting-principles.deep_explanation: Counting Principles is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Permutations, combinations, pigeonhole
counting-principles.exam_tip: Practice Counting Principles problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

probability-basics.label: Probability Basics
probability-basics.deep_explanation: Probability Basics is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Axioms, conditional, Bayes theorem
common_misconceptions: Students compute P(A|B) as P(B|A) because they swap the conditional direction
Students forget to normalize by the total probability in Bayes' theorem because they skip the denominator
Students assume P(A|B) + P(A|B^c) = 1 because they confuse it with P(A) + P(A^c) = 1
Students treat independent events as mutually exclusive because both 'decouple' events intuitively
Students add probabilities of overlapping events without subtracting the intersection because inclusion-exclusion feels unnecessary
probability-basics.exam_tip: Practice Probability Basics problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

random-variables.label: Random Variables
random-variables.deep_explanation: Random Variables is a foundational topic in probability statistics. PMF, PDF, CDF, expectation, variance
random-variables.exam_tip: Practice Random Variables problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

discrete-distributions.label: Discrete Distributions
discrete-distributions.deep_explanation: Discrete Distributions is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Binomial, Poisson, geometric, hypergeometric
discrete-distributions.exam_tip: Practice Discrete Distributions problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

continuous-distributions.label: Continuous Distributions
continuous-distributions.deep_explanation: Continuous Distributions is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Normal, exponential, uniform, gamma
common_misconceptions: Students compute P(X = a) as nonzero for continuous distributions because they're used to discrete PMFs
Students integrate the CDF instead of the PDF to get probability because they confuse the roles
Students forget that total area under the PDF must equal 1 because they don't normalize
Students treat the PDF value f(x) as a probability because the name 'density function' misleads them
Students use the wrong bounds when computing P(a < X < b) for mixed-discrete-continuous because they don't separate cases
continuous-distributions.exam_tip: Practice Continuous Distributions problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

joint-distributions.label: Joint Distributions
joint-distributions.deep_explanation: Joint Distributions is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Joint, marginal, conditional distributions, independence
joint-distributions.exam_tip: Practice Joint Distributions problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

hypothesis-testing.label: Hypothesis Testing
hypothesis-testing.canonical_definition: Type I/II errors, p-values, confidence intervals
hypothesis-testing.deep_explanation: Hypothesis Testing is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Type I/II errors, p-values, confidence intervals
hypothesis-testing.exam_tip: Practice Hypothesis Testing problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

regression-correlation.label: Regression & Correlation
regression-correlation.deep_explanation: Regression & Correlation is a foundational topic in probability statistics. Linear regression, correlation coefficient
regression-correlation.exam_tip: Practice Regression & Correlation problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

complex-numbers.label: Complex Numbers
complex-numbers.canonical_definition: Algebra, polar form, De Moivre's theorem
complex-numbers.deep_explanation: Complex Numbers is a foundational topic in complex variables. Algebra, polar form, De Moivre's theorem
complex-numbers.exam_tip: Practice Complex Numbers problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

analytic-functions.label: Analytic Functions
analytic-functions.deep_explanation: Analytic Functions is a foundational topic in complex variables. Cauchy-Riemann equations, harmonic functions
analytic-functions.exam_tip: Practice Analytic Functions problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

complex-integration.label: Complex Integration
complex-integration.deep_explanation: Complex Integration is a foundational topic in complex variables. Contour integrals, Cauchy integral theorem/formula
common_misconceptions: Students verify only the first of the two Cauchy-Riemann equations before calling a function holomorphic because they stop at the first check
Students apply Cauchy's theorem when the contour encloses a singularity because they forget to check analyticity inside the contour
Students compute residues at essential singularities using the pole formula because they treat all singularities the same
Students forget to pick the correct branch when integrating around multi-valued functions because they use the principal branch automatically
Students apply the residue theorem to non-closed contours because they overgeneralize the scope
complex-integration.exam_tip: Practice Complex Integration problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

taylor-laurent.label: Taylor & Laurent Series
taylor-laurent.deep_explanation: Taylor & Laurent Series is a foundational topic in complex variables. Series expansions, singularities classification
common_misconceptions: Students drop the remainder term when computing approximations because they think the series is exact
Students compute Taylor series about x = 0 when the problem asks about x = a because they default to Maclaurin
Students forget to evaluate the n-th derivative at the expansion point because they leave f⁽ⁿ⁾(x) in the coefficient
Students assume the Taylor series converges everywhere because the formula looks universal
Students confuse the Taylor series of f with its Taylor polynomial because both share the same formula for finite n
taylor-laurent.exam_tip: Practice Taylor & Laurent Series problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

residue-calculus.label: Residue Calculus
residue-calculus.deep_explanation: Residue Calculus is a foundational topic in complex variables. Residue theorem, real integral evaluation
residue-calculus.exam_tip: Practice Residue Calculus problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

conformal-mapping.label: Conformal Mapping
conformal-mapping.deep_explanation: Conformal Mapping is a foundational topic in complex variables. Bilinear transformations, Joukowski
conformal-mapping.exam_tip: Practice Conformal Mapping problems at low frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

root-finding.label: Root Finding
root-finding.deep_explanation: Root Finding is a foundational topic in numerical methods. Bisection, Newton-Raphson, secant, fixed-point
root-finding.exam_tip: Practice Root Finding problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

interpolation.label: Interpolation
interpolation.deep_explanation: Interpolation is a foundational topic in numerical methods. Lagrange, Newton divided differences, splines
interpolation.exam_tip: Practice Interpolation problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

numerical-integration.label: Numerical Integration
numerical-integration.deep_explanation: Numerical Integration is a foundational topic in numerical methods. Trapezoidal, Simpson's, Gaussian quadrature
numerical-integration.exam_tip: Practice Numerical Integration problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

numerical-ode.label: Numerical ODE Solvers
numerical-ode.deep_explanation: Numerical ODE Solvers is a foundational topic in numerical methods. Euler, Runge-Kutta, stability analysis
numerical-ode.exam_tip: Practice Numerical ODE Solvers problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

numerical-linear-algebra.label: Numerical Linear Algebra
numerical-linear-algebra.canonical_definition: LU decomposition, iterative methods, condition number
numerical-linear-algebra.deep_explanation: Numerical Linear Algebra is a foundational topic in numerical methods. LU decomposition, iterative methods, condition number
numerical-linear-algebra.exam_tip: Practice Numerical Linear Algebra problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

laplace-transform.label: Laplace Transform
laplace-transform.deep_explanation: Laplace Transform is a foundational topic in transform theory. Definition, properties, standard transforms
common_misconceptions: Students forget the shift property L{e^(at) f(t)} = F(s - a) and apply L{f(t)} directly because they ignore the exponential
Students use the initial value from the problem instead of 0 in the derivative property because they confuse the formula
Students apply the inverse Laplace transform term-by-term without partial-fraction decomposition because they hope the transform is additive
Students compute L{f(t)g(t)} as F(s)·G(s) because convolution-in-time (not product-in-time) corresponds to multiplication-in-frequency
Students try to invert F(s) = 1/(s² + 2s + 5) directly because they don't complete the square first
prerequisite_reminders: Review ode second order nonhomo first.
laplace-transform.exam_tip: Practice Laplace Transform problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

inverse-laplace.label: Inverse Laplace Transform
inverse-laplace.deep_explanation: Inverse Laplace Transform is a foundational topic in transform theory. Partial fractions method, convolution
inverse-laplace.exam_tip: Practice Inverse Laplace Transform problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

laplace-applications.label: Laplace Applications
laplace-applications.canonical_definition: Solving ODEs, circuit analysis, transfer functions
laplace-applications.deep_explanation: Laplace Applications is a foundational topic in transform theory. Solving ODEs, circuit analysis, transfer functions
laplace-applications.exam_tip: Practice Laplace Applications problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

fourier-series.label: Fourier Series
fourier-series.deep_explanation: Fourier Series is a foundational topic in transform theory. Trigonometric series, Dirichlet conditions, Parseval
fourier-series.exam_tip: Practice Fourier Series problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

fourier-transform.label: Fourier Transform
fourier-transform.deep_explanation: Fourier Transform is a foundational topic in transform theory. FT properties, convolution, applications
fourier-transform.exam_tip: Practice Fourier Transform problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

z-transform.label: Z-Transform
z-transform.deep_explanation: Z-Transform is a foundational topic in transform theory. Definition, properties, inverse, difference equations
z-transform.exam_tip: Practice Z-Transform problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

vector-fields.label: Vector Fields
vector-fields.deep_explanation: Vector Fields is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Scalar/vector fields, gradient
vector-fields.exam_tip: Practice Vector Fields problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

divergence-curl.label: Divergence & Curl
divergence-curl.deep_explanation: Divergence & Curl is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Divergence, curl, Laplacian, identities
divergence-curl.exam_tip: Practice Divergence & Curl problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

line-integrals.label: Line Integrals
line-integrals.deep_explanation: Line Integrals is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Scalar/vector line integrals, work, circulation
line-integrals.exam_tip: Practice Line Integrals problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

surface-integrals.label: Surface Integrals
surface-integrals.deep_explanation: Surface Integrals is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Flux, surface parameterization
surface-integrals.exam_tip: Practice Surface Integrals problems at medium frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

greens-theorem.label: Green's Theorem
greens-theorem.deep_explanation: Green's Theorem is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Circulation-flux form, applications
greens-theorem.exam_tip: Practice Green's Theorem problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

stokes-theorem.label: Stokes' Theorem
stokes-theorem.deep_explanation: Stokes' Theorem is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Generalized curl theorem, applications
stokes-theorem.exam_tip: Practice Stokes' Theorem problems at high frequency matching GATE exam distribution.

gauss-divergence.label: Gauss Divergence Theorem
gauss-divergence.deep_explanation: Gauss Divergence Theorem is a foundational topic in vector calculus. Volume-surface flux relationship
